title: Navigating the City
opening: Excuse me, you seem a bit disoriented. May I be of assistance?

t1.prompt: Excuse me, you seem a bit disoriented. May I be of assistance?
t1.promptTranslation: Excuse me, you seem a bit disoriented. May I be of assistance? — Excuse me, you look a bit lost. Can I help you?

t2.prompt: Of course, I'd be glad to lend a hand. Where exactly are you trying to get to?
t2.promptTranslation: Of course, I'd be glad to lend a hand. Where exactly are you trying to get to? — Yes, I'll help. Where do you want to go?

t3_specific_place.prompt: Ah, the National Gallery! A splendid choice. From here, you'll want to head straight down this street. Trafalgar Square will loom large directly ahead of you, and the Gallery is on its north side. It's a brisk 15-minute walk, or you can hop on any bus heading to Charing Cross.
t3_specific_place.promptTranslation: Ah, the National Gallery! A splendid choice. From here, you'll want to head straight down this street. Trafalgar Square will loom large directly ahead of you, and the Gallery is on its north side. It's a brisk 15-minute walk, or you can hop on any bus heading to Charing Cross. — The National Gallery! Go straight down this street. Trafalgar Square is ahead, the Gallery is on its north side. It's a 15-minute walk, or take a bus to Charing Cross.

t3_suggestion.prompt: Well, given our current location, I'd highly recommend a visit to the British Museum. It's absolutely phenomenal, and not too far from here. It's about a 30-minute walk, or a quick tube ride on the Central Line.
t3_suggestion.promptTranslation: Well, given our current location, I'd highly recommend a visit to the British Museum. It's absolutely phenomenal, and not too far from here. It's about a 30-minute walk, or a quick tube ride on the Central Line. — From here, I suggest the British Museum. It's great and nearby. It's a 30-minute walk, or a short tube ride.

t4_details.prompt: To elaborate, the bus stop for Charing Cross is just a stone's throw away, directly across the street. Any bus displaying 'Charing Cross' or 'Westminster' will serve your purpose. If walking, simply keep the impressive architecture of St Martin-in-the-Fields church on your left as you approach the Square.
t4_details.promptTranslation: To elaborate, the bus stop for Charing Cross is just a stone's throw away, directly across the street. Any bus displaying 'Charing Cross' or 'Westminster' will serve your purpose. If walking, simply keep the impressive architecture of St Martin-in-the-Fields church on your left as you approach the Square. — The bus stop for Charing Cross is very close, across the street. Take any bus going to Charing Cross or Westminster. If walking, keep St Martin-in-the-Fields church on your left as you get to the Square.

t4_details_museum.prompt: For the British Museum, the closest tube station is Tottenham Court Road, about a five-minute walk from here. From there, you'll want the Central Line, just two stops to Russell Square, which is practically on the museum's doorstep. It's significantly quicker than walking.
t4_details_museum.promptTranslation: For the British Museum, the closest tube station is Tottenham Court Road, about a five-minute walk from here. From there, you'll want the Central Line, just two stops to Russell Square, which is practically on the museum's doorstep. It's significantly quicker than walking. — For the British Museum, Tottenham Court Road tube station is 5 minutes walk. Take the Central Line, two stops to Russell Square, right by the museum. It's much faster.

t4_alt_suggestion.prompt: Alright, if the museum isn't quite your cup of tea, how about a stroll through Hyde Park? It's a vast green oasis perfect for unwinding. It's a bit of a trek on foot, but easily accessible by tube from Green Park or Hyde Park Corner.
t4_alt_suggestion.promptTranslation: Alright, if the museum isn't quite your cup of tea, how about a stroll through Hyde Park? It's a vast green oasis perfect for unwinding. It's a bit of a trek on foot, but easily accessible by tube from Green Park or Hyde Park Corner. — Okay, if you don't like the museum, how about Hyde Park? It's a big park, good for relaxing. It's far to walk but easy by tube from Green Park or Hyde Park Corner.

t-repeat.prompt: I beg your pardon? Could you possibly reiterate that for me?
t-repeat.promptTranslation: I beg your pardon? Could you possibly reiterate that for me? — Sorry? Could you say that again?

t-end.prompt: You're most welcome! Enjoy your visit to London, and do take care.
t-end.promptTranslation: You're most welcome! Enjoy your visit to London, and do take care. — You're welcome! Enjoy London, and be careful.
